questions.stem: The book ______ by many students in our school every year.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语The book是动作read的承受者，应用被动语态（be+过去分词）。由every year可知用一般现在时，主语是单数，所以用is read。B是主动语态第三人称单数；C是主动语态原形；D是一般过去时被动语态，与every year时间状语不符。解题技巧：看到by短语首先考虑被动语态，再根据时间状语确定时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —______ you ______ your homework yet? —Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。yet是现在完成时的标志词，问句应用Have you finished；ago是一般过去时的标志词，答句应用finished。A项第一空错误，did与yet不搭配；C项第二空错误，ago不能与现在完成时连用；D项两个空都错误。解题技巧：yet/ever/never/already常与现在完成时连用；ago/yesterday/last week与一般过去时连用。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: This is the school ______ I studied three years ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系词的选择。先行词是the school（地点），从句I studied three years ago中缺少地点状语（in the school），应用where引导。A项which在从句中作主语或宾语，但study不及物时不需要宾语；B项who指人；D项when指时间。解题技巧：看先行词是人还是物，再看从句缺什么成分——缺主语/宾语用which/that，缺状语用where/when。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door? —It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing for a meeting.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情态动词表推测。由He has gone to Beijing可知他去北京了（还没回来），所以不可能是他敲门。否定推测用can't（不可能）。A项must表示肯定推测'一定是'，与语境矛盾；B项might表示'可能是'，语气不确定，但语境是确定不是；D项should表示'应该'，不表推测。解题技巧：must表肯定推测'一定是'，can't表否定推测'不可能是'，might表不确定'可能是'，根据语境证据选择。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It is ______ to see so many changes in my hometown.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换及形容词辨析。be动词后接形容词作表语，排除A（动词）和D（名词）。exciting表示'令人兴奋的'，修饰事物；excited表示'感到兴奋的'，修饰人。本句主语是It，指代'看到家乡变化'这件事，应用exciting。解题技巧：-ing形容词修饰事物（令人...的），-ed形容词修饰人（感到...的）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词词义辨析及搭配。be satisfied with是固定搭配，意为'对...满意'。根据句意'如果你满足于当前分数，就应该更努力'符合逻辑。A和D通常不修饰人；B项excited应与about/with连用，但'对分数感到兴奋'与后文'应该更努力'逻辑矛盾。解题技巧：根据后文'work harder'可推断前文是'满足现状'，satisfied符合语境。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ old clothes instead of throwing them away.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。give away表示'捐赠，赠送'，符合句意'我们应该把旧衣服捐出去而不是扔掉'。A项put on（穿上）；B项take off（脱下，起飞）；D项try on（试穿）均与'instead of throwing them away'的环保语境不符。解题技巧：根据语境'不扔掉'推断是'捐赠'，give away有'无偿给予他人'的含义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。be interested in是固定短语，意为'对...感兴趣'。其他介词与interested搭配不当。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project to help protect the environment. The project encouraged students to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and old newspapers.

At first, many students were not interested (8)______ it. They thought it was boring and (9)______ too much time. However, after our science teacher Mr. Li explained (10)______ recycling could save energy and reduce pollution, more and more students joined in.

My classmate Tom and I decided to work together. We put a big box in our classroom and asked everyone to put their empty bottles in it. Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to the recycling center near our school. Although it was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the box (12)______ of bottles.

By the end of the month, our class had collected over 500 bottles! The headmaster praised us in the morning meeting and said we were excellent role models for other students. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词辨析。主语是it（指代recycling），表示'花费时间'用take，结构为it takes/took time。spend的主语是人（sb spends time）；cost通常指花费金钱，主语是物；pay指支付金钱。此处it took too much time符合语法和语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project to help protect the environment. The project encouraged students to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and old newspapers.

At first, many students were not interested (8)______ it. They thought it was boring and (9)______ too much time. However, after our science teacher Mr. Li explained (10)______ recycling could save energy and reduce pollution, more and more students joined in.

My classmate Tom and I decided to work together. We put a big box in our classroom and asked everyone to put their empty bottles in it. Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to the recycling center near our school. Although it was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the box (12)______ of bottles.

By the end of the month, our class had collected over 500 bottles! The headmaster praised us in the morning meeting and said we were excellent role models for other students. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句连接词。根据句意'李老师解释回收'如何'节约能源和减少污染'，应用how表示方式。why表示原因（为什么），when表示时间（何时），where表示地点（何地），均不符合语境逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project to help protect the environment. The project encouraged students to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and old newspapers.

At first, many students were not interested (8)______ it. They thought it was boring and (9)______ too much time. However, after our science teacher Mr. Li explained (10)______ recycling could save energy and reduce pollution, more and more students joined in.

My classmate Tom and I decided to work together. We put a big box in our classroom and asked everyone to put their empty bottles in it. Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to the recycling center near our school. Although it was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the box (12)______ of bottles.

By the end of the month, our class had collected over 500 bottles! The headmaster praised us in the morning meeting and said we were excellent role models for other students. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词时态和词义。文章整体使用一般过去时（started, encouraged, thought等），此处应用过去式took。take...to...表示'把...带到...'。B项bring是原形且方向相反（bring表示带来）；C项carried侧重'搬运'重物，但此处只是带瓶子去回收站；D项sent表示'邮寄，发送'，不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project to help protect the environment. The project encouraged students to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and old newspapers.

At first, many students were not interested (8)______ it. They thought it was boring and (9)______ too much time. However, after our science teacher Mr. Li explained (10)______ recycling could save energy and reduce pollution, more and more students joined in.

My classmate Tom and I decided to work together. We put a big box in our classroom and asked everyone to put their empty bottles in it. Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to the recycling center near our school. Although it was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the box (12)______ of bottles.

By the end of the month, our class had collected over 500 bottles! The headmaster praised us in the morning meeting and said we were excellent role models for other students. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。full of是固定短语，意为'充满...'，此处作后置定语修饰box，相当于which was full of bottles。filled需与with搭配（filled with）；fill是动词原形；filling是现在分词，表示主动，但box是被装满，逻辑不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project to help protect the environment. The project encouraged students to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and old newspapers.

At first, many students were not interested (8)______ it. They thought it was boring and (9)______ too much time. However, after our science teacher Mr. Li explained (10)______ recycling could save energy and reduce pollution, more and more students joined in.

My classmate Tom and I decided to work together. We put a big box in our classroom and asked everyone to put their empty bottles in it. Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to the recycling center near our school. Although it was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the box (12)______ of bottles.

By the end of the month, our class had collected over 500 bottles! The headmaster praised us in the morning meeting and said we were excellent role models for other students. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.stem: What did the scientists find in the experiment mentioned in Paragraph 2?
options: A. Sleeping less helps students study better.
B. Students who slept well remembered 40% more information.
C. The second group slept for eight hours after studying.
D. Both groups remembered the same amount of information.
questions.explanation: 本题考查细节理解。根据第二段'They found that the students who slept well remembered 40% more than those who didn't sleep'可知，睡眠好的学生比没睡觉的学生多记住40%的信息。A项与原文意思相反；C项错误，第二组是stayed awake all night（熬夜）；D项与原文矛盾。解题技巧：细节题要精准定位原文，注意数字和比较级。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students think sleeping less gives them more time to study. However, new research shows that good sleep is actually very important for learning and memory.

Scientists at a university did an experiment with two groups of students. The first group slept for eight hours after studying new information, while the second group stayed awake all night. The next day, the scientists tested both groups. They found that the students who slept well remembered 40% more than those who didn't sleep.

Why does sleep help with memory? During sleep, our brains work to organize and store the information we learned during the day. It's like saving files on a computer. If you don't sleep enough, your brain doesn't have time to complete this task.

Dr. Smith, a sleep expert, suggests that students should form good sleeping habits. "Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps your body clock work better," he says. He also recommends avoiding screen time before bed because the blue light from phones and computers makes it harder to fall asleep.

So, if you want to do better in school, don't sacrifice your sleep for extra study time. A good night's sleep might be the secret weapon for your academic success.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Smith's suggestions in Paragraph 4?
options: A. Students should use computers more before bed.
B. Irregular sleep patterns don't affect study.
C. Blue light helps people fall asleep quickly.
D. Regular sleep schedule is good for health and study.
questions.explanation: 本题考查推理判断。根据第四段Dr. Smith建议'每天在同一时间睡觉和起床有助于生物钟更好地工作'以及避免睡前看屏幕，可推断规律的作息对健康和学习有益。A项与原文'avoiding screen time'相反；B项与'helps your body clock work better'矛盾；C项与'makes it harder to fall asleep'相反。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能选与原文矛盾的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students think sleeping less gives them more time to study. However, new research shows that good sleep is actually very important for learning and memory.

Scientists at a university did an experiment with two groups of students. The first group slept for eight hours after studying new information, while the second group stayed awake all night. The next day, the scientists tested both groups. They found that the students who slept well remembered 40% more than those who didn't sleep.

Why does sleep help with memory? During sleep, our brains work to organize and store the information we learned during the day. It's like saving files on a computer. If you don't sleep enough, your brain doesn't have time to complete this task.

Dr. Smith, a sleep expert, suggests that students should form good sleeping habits. "Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps your body clock work better," he says. He also recommends avoiding screen time before bed because the blue light from phones and computers makes it harder to fall asleep.

So, if you want to do better in school, don't sacrifice your sleep for extra study time. A good night's sleep might be the secret weapon for your academic success.

questions.stem: What does the word "sacrifice" in the last paragraph probably mean?
options: A. To give up something important for something else
B. To share something with others
D. To increase the amount of something
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'不要为了额外的学习时间而sacrifice睡眠'，结合前文强调睡眠重要性，可知sacrifice意为'牺牲'，即放弃某物以换取另一物。B项'分享'、C项'保护'、D项'增加'均不符合语境。解题技巧：通过上下文逻辑（sleep与extra study time的取舍关系）猜测词义，注意转折词but/however。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students think sleeping less gives them more time to study. However, new research shows that good sleep is actually very important for learning and memory.

Scientists at a university did an experiment with two groups of students. The first group slept for eight hours after studying new information, while the second group stayed awake all night. The next day, the scientists tested both groups. They found that the students who slept well remembered 40% more than those who didn't sleep.

Why does sleep help with memory? During sleep, our brains work to organize and store the information we learned during the day. It's like saving files on a computer. If you don't sleep enough, your brain doesn't have time to complete this task.

Dr. Smith, a sleep expert, suggests that students should form good sleeping habits. "Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps your body clock work better," he says. He also recommends avoiding screen time before bed because the blue light from phones and computers makes it harder to fall asleep.

So, if you want to do better in school, don't sacrifice your sleep for extra study time. A good night's sleep might be the secret weapon for your academic success.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Finish Homework Quickly
B. The Bad Effects of Staying Up Late
C. Sleep: The Secret to Better Learning
D. Why Students Need Less Sleep
questions.explanation: 本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章开篇指出睡眠对学习记忆的重要性，通过实验说明睡眠好的学生记忆更好，解释原因并给出建议，最后总结睡眠是学业成功的秘密武器。C项准确概括全文核心。A项仅在开头提及，非重点；B项只涉及熬夜的负面影响，片面；D项与原文观点相反。解题技巧：最佳标题应覆盖全文主要内容，出现频率高的关键词（sleep/memory/learning）应体现在标题中。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students think sleeping less gives them more time to study. However, new research shows that good sleep is actually very important for learning and memory.

Scientists at a university did an experiment with two groups of students. The first group slept for eight hours after studying new information, while the second group stayed awake all night. The next day, the scientists tested both groups. They found that the students who slept well remembered 40% more than those who didn't sleep.

Why does sleep help with memory? During sleep, our brains work to organize and store the information we learned during the day. It's like saving files on a computer. If you don't sleep enough, your brain doesn't have time to complete this task.

Dr. Smith, a sleep expert, suggests that students should form good sleeping habits. "Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps your body clock work better," he says. He also recommends avoiding screen time before bed because the blue light from phones and computers makes it harder to fall asleep.

So, if you want to do better in school, don't sacrifice your sleep for extra study time. A good night's sleep might be the secret weapon for your academic success.

options: A. First, save energy at home.
B. This not only reduces air pollution but also saves money.
C. Third, reuse things instead of throwing them away.
D. Single-use plastics are a major source of pollution.
E. However, it is difficult to change our habits.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落主题句及连接词。后文出现For example列举关灯、拔掉插头等节约用电的例子，且下一段以Second开头，可知此处应是First引出的第一点建议。A项'首先，在家节约能源'符合逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people want to live a greener life, but they don't know where to start. Here are some simple ways to help protect the environment.

(17)______ For example, turn off the lights when you leave a room and unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. This can save a lot of electricity.

Second, reduce waste by using reusable bags and bottles. (18)______ Plastic bags and bottles take hundreds of years to break down in nature. By using reusable items, we can create less trash.

(19)______ Many old items can be given a second life. For instance, you can turn old clothes into cleaning rags or use glass jars to store food.

Finally, try to walk or ride a bike instead of taking a car for short distances. (20)______ It also helps you stay fit and healthy.

options: A. First, save energy at home.
B. This not only reduces air pollution but also saves money.
C. Third, reuse things instead of throwing them away.
D. Single-use plastics are a major source of pollution.
E. However, it is difficult to change our habits.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系及代词指代。后文'塑料袋和瓶子需要数百年才能在大自然中分解'是对前文的解释说明，D项'一次性塑料是主要的污染源'引出后文对塑料危害的解释，且Single-use plastics与前文reusable bags形成对比。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people want to live a greener life, but they don't know where to start. Here are some simple ways to help protect the environment.

(17)______ For example, turn off the lights when you leave a room and unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. This can save a lot of electricity.

Second, reduce waste by using reusable bags and bottles. (18)______ Plastic bags and bottles take hundreds of years to break down in nature. By using reusable items, we can create less trash.

(19)______ Many old items can be given a second life. For instance, you can turn old clothes into cleaning rags or use glass jars to store food.

Finally, try to walk or ride a bike instead of taking a car for short distances. (20)______ It also helps you stay fit and healthy.

options: A. First, save energy at home.
B. This not only reduces air pollution but also saves money.
C. Third, reuse things instead of throwing them away.
D. Single-use plastics are a major source of pollution.
E. However, it is difficult to change our habits.
questions.explanation: 本题考查顺序连接词。前文有First、Second，后文有Finally，此处应是Third引出的第三点建议。后文'给旧物品第二次生命'对应C项'reuse things（重复使用物品）'。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people want to live a greener life, but they don't know where to start. Here are some simple ways to help protect the environment.

(17)______ For example, turn off the lights when you leave a room and unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. This can save a lot of electricity.

Second, reduce waste by using reusable bags and bottles. (18)______ Plastic bags and bottles take hundreds of years to break down in nature. By using reusable items, we can create less trash.

(19)______ Many old items can be given a second life. For instance, you can turn old clothes into cleaning rags or use glass jars to store food.

Finally, try to walk or ride a bike instead of taking a car for short distances. (20)______ It also helps you stay fit and healthy.

options: A. First, save energy at home.
B. This not only reduces air pollution but also saves money.
C. Third, reuse things instead of throwing them away.
D. Single-use plastics are a major source of pollution.
E. However, it is difficult to change our habits.
questions.explanation: 本题考查递进关系。后文'It also helps...'（还有助于...）中的also表明前文应有类似的not only结构，形成not only...also...的递进关系。B项'这不仅减少空气污染还能省钱'与后文'还有助于保持健康'构成并列的好处。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

(17)______ For example, turn off the lights when you leave a room and unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. This can save a lot of electricity.

Second, reduce waste by using reusable bags and bottles. (18)______ Plastic bags and bottles take hundreds of years to break down in nature. By using reusable items, we can create less trash.

(19)______ Many old items can be given a second life. For instance, you can turn old clothes into cleaning rags or use glass jars to store food.

Finally, try to walk or ride a bike instead of taking a car for short distances. (20)______ It also helps you stay fit and healthy.
